svg.text: YORUBA
HISTORY & WEST AFRICA
IFE AND THE YORUBA KINGDOMS
Ile-Ife: spiritual capital; Yoruba trace civilization here
~500-1000 CE: Ife terracotta sculptures — masterworks of African art
Oyo Empire (~1300-1836): major West African political power
Oyo cavalry: dominated trade routes from forest to savanna
Benin Kingdom (adj.): Yoruba-influenced brass casting tradition
SLAVE TRADE & DIASPORA
Yoruba: one of most heavily enslaved African groups
Yoruba religion: Candomble (Brazil) • Santeria (Cuba) • Vodou (Haiti)
Orishas (Yoruba deities) still worshipped across Americas
~50M speakers; Nigeria’s largest ethnic group
TWI / AKAN HISTORY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
ASHANTI EMPIRE
Akan people: forest region of modern Ghana; gold-rich territory
~1701: Osei Tutu founds Ashanti Confederacy (Asante Empire)
Golden Stool (Sika Dwa): sacred symbol of Ashanti unity and soul
Ashanti resisted British annexation 4 times (1823-1900)
AKAN GOLD AND SLAVE TRADE
Gold Coast (Ghana): named for Akan gold wealth (British colony)
Akan also participated in Atlantic slave trade (as captors + captives)
Akan cultural influence in Caribbean: Anansi spider stories
Kwame Nkrumah: Akan; first president of Ghana (1957 independence)
TWI TODAY
~9M speakers; Twi (Asante dialect) most widely spoken Akan form
Ghana: Akan group ~47% of population; Twi = informal lingua franca
YORUBA-AKAN: WEST AFRICAN SIBLINGS
Both: ancient kingdoms with sophisticated political systems
Both: deeply impacted by Atlantic slave trade
Both: living religious traditions in the Americas
Anansi (Akan trickster) and Eshu/Exu (Yoruba trickster): parallel gods
Two great West African civilizations: gold, iron, art, diaspora
PLATE 30d ALT · YORUBA / TWI · HISTORY
